description: Conformance vectors for composition_ref derivation, including key_source extension.

vectors.description: Canonical composition artifact without key_source. Hash must match spec derivation example.
key_source=inline — self-certifying path (did:key, did:aps). No resolver on hot path, SSRF eliminated. Produces a distinct composition_ref from baseline.
key_source=resolver — key fetched from remote resolver at verification time. Allowlist profile required. key_source in signed artifact makes resolution method auditable across the record lifetime.
key_source=cache without a prior key_source=resolver event in the chain. Hash recomputes correctly but provenance chain is broken — no population parent. Non-conformant: verifier MUST reject.
key_source=resolver with resolver_url outside the declared allowlist. Evidence layer failure independent of wire signature — L1 may pass, L2 MUST reject. Non-conformant.

revocation_check_at_ms (1748000000000) precedes authority_verified_at_ms (1748087390000) — dual-timestamp invariant violated (authority_verified_at_ms <= revocation_check_at_ms required). L1 passes; L2 rejects on temporal gap. Non-conformant.

key_source=inline but no key_origin_proof or key_origin_type present. The signer controls both key material and source label — the verifier cannot distinguish a genuinely pinned inline key from a resolver-origin key relabeled as inline. Non-conformant: verifier MUST reject.

key_source=inline with key_origin_type=self-generated and key_origin_proof (SHA-256 of the self-signed creation statement). Verifier confirms: creation timestamp predates first use, no resolver fetch event in receipt chain for this key. Conformant: verifier MUST accept.

key_source=cache with cache_parent_ref pointing to the prior key_source=resolver event (composition_ref ac5ad545... from vector key_source-resolver). Provenance chain intact — population event audited, cache hit references back. Conformant: verifier MUST accept.

notes: key_source enters the JCS field set — stripping it before hashing produces a wrong composition_ref.
baseline and key_source-inline use identical fields except for key_source presence; their hashes differ by construction.
hash_algo: SHA-256(JCS(composition_artifact)) where JCS is RFC 8785 (sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False).
Non-conformant vectors carry conformant=false and failure_mode. Hash still recomputes — the failure is semantic, not hash-level.

resolver-to-cache-valid: cache_parent_ref references the composition_ref of the key_source=resolver population event (vector key_source-resolver). A verifier traces the chain to confirm the cache hit is grounded.
stale-revocation: revocation_check_at_ms < authority_verified_at_ms violates the invariant authority_verified_at_ms <= revocation_check_at_ms <= action_timestamp_ms.

inline-no-origin-proof: key_source=inline without key_origin_proof is non-conformant — the enum is bypassable if the signer controls both key material and source label. A verifier cannot distinguish a genuinely pinned inline key from a resolver-origin key relabeled as inline.

inline-with-origin-attestation: key_origin_type ∈ {self-generated, operator-pinned, resolver-attested, hardware-bound}. key_origin_proof = SHA-256 of the signed creation or pinning statement. Verifier checks: creation timestamp predates first use, no resolver fetch event in receipt chain for this key material.

inline vs cache vs resolver: three provenance profiles, not an enum of trust levels. resolver proves through allowlist + fetch evidence. cache proves through a parent population event (cache_parent_ref). inline proves through origin attestation (key_origin_proof + key_origin_type).
